mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly increasing present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.5 percent signals labor market deterioration and elevated recession probability, which compresses corporate earnings forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in greater than 55 percent of instances when the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer demand, reducing revenue expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts yield positive forward returns in greater than 50 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2 percent annualized confirms strong supply-side momentum and capacity utilization, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth exceeding 2 percent annualized correlates with negative 5-day returns in greater than 60 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with a stagnant federal funds rate indicates negative real rates and policy lag, forcing markets to price in aggressive future tightening and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Real rate compression periods show positive 60-day returns in greater than 55 percent of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect sustained economic expansion and rising household income, which directly boosts corporate revenue projections and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth periods yield negative forward returns in greater than 50 percent of cases, or the signal decays to zero IC within 10 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent annualized signals economic stagnation and weakening aggregate demand, which reduces earnings growth expectations and causes negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth below 1.5 percent annualized correlates with positive 60-day returns in greater than 60 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production declines while total payrolls increase indicates labor hoarding and deteriorating productivity, which erodes corporate efficiency and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Productivity divergence periods show positive 5-day returns in greater than 55 percent of cases, or the joint effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.
